LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT AVRAHAM SARANGA'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT'S ANSWER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. CROSS-COMPLAINANT AVRAHAM SARANGA'S DEMURRER TO ALL 26 AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES IN CROSS-DEFENDANT CHERYL LUNA'S ANSWER TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS) WHILE THE FILING OF A DEMURRER TO AN ANSWER IS NOT SANCTIONABLE CONDUCT AS THERE IS A BASIS FOR SUCH A FILING PER FPI DEVELOPMENT, INC. V. NAKASHIMA (1991) 231 CAL.APP.3D 346, THE FILING OF THIS DEMURRER IS A COMPLETE WASTE OF TIME AND RULING UPON IT DOES NOTHING TO FURTHER THE LITIGATION IN THIS CASE. THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES ARE ALLEGED IN A WAY THAT CONFORMS TO LONG-ESTABLISHED AND ACCEPTED PRACTICE IN THE BAY AREA STATE COURTS. THE PURPOSE OF THIS PRACTICE IS TO PRESERVE A DEFENDANT'S OPTIONS UNTIL THE DEFENDANT IS ABLE TO LEARN MORE ABOUT THE PLAINTIFF'S CASE. THE PROPER AND WELL-ACCEPTED PROCEDURE FOR A PLAINTIFF WHO CONFRONTS AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES SUCH AS THOSE ALLEGED BY MS. LUNA IS, AFTER A REASONABLE TIME HAS ELAPSED, TO SERVE FORM INTERROGATORY 15.1 AND, IF NO INFORMATION IS PROVIDED AS TO PARTICULAR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, SEEK A STIPULATION THAT THOSE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES ARE WITHDRAWN OR, FAILING THAT, FILE A MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. COUNSEL FOR MS. LUNA IS ADMONISHED THAT HER FILING OF A SUR-REPLY WAS NOT PERMITTED. COUNSEL FOR MS. LUNA TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M.GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion For An Order That The Truth Of Matters Be Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,365.00 (Per Judge Kahn On 10/1/18)
Off Calendar Oct-02-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Purtell, Esq. 415-722-6291 for Plaintiff Cheryl Luna.
Jennifer Lim, Esq. 415-877-4486 of Rencher Law Group for Defendant Avraham Saranga.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces the tentative ruling to Deny. The motion is entirely based on the failure of Ms Luna to provide verification of responses. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT AVRAHAM SARANGA'S Motion For An Order That The Truth Of Matters Be Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,365.00.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Denied.

Counsel for Ms. Luna to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-02-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Purtell, Esq. 415-722-6291 for Plaintiff Cheryl Luna.
Jennifer Lim, Esq. 415-877-4486 of Rencher Law Group for Defendant Avraham Saranga.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT AVRAHAM SARANGA'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT'S ANSWER TO CROSS COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Cross-complainant Avraham Saranga's demurrer to all 26 affirmative defenses in cross-defendant Cheryl Luna's answer to the cross-complaint is overruled in its entirety. While the filing of a demurrer to an answer is not sanctionable conduct as there is a basis for such a filing per FPI Development, Inc. v. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 346, the filing of this demurrer is a complete waste of time and ruling upon it does nothing to further the litigation in this case. The affirmative defenses are alleged in a way that conforms to long-established and accepted practice in the Bay Area state courts. The purpose of this practice is to preserve a defendant's options until the defendant is able to learn more about the plaintiff's case. The proper and well-accepted procedure for a plaintiff who confronts affirmative defenses such as those alleged by Ms. Luna is, after a reasonable time has elapsed, to serve form interrogatory 15.1 and, if no information is provided as to particular affirmative defenses, seek a stipulation that those affirmative defenses are withdrawn or, failing that, file a motion for summary adjudication. Counsel for Ms. Luna is admonished that her filing of a sur-reply was not permitted.

Counsel for Ms. Luna to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares his views. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT AVRAHAM SARANGA'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.

Defendant Avraham Saranga's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in and the entirety of the complaint filed by plaintiff Cheryl Luna is denied in its entirety. Off Calendar Jul-12-2018 Continued To Sep-12-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
Off Calendar Jun-19-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Purtell, Esq. 415-722-6291 for Plaintiff Cheryl Luna.
Jennifer Lim, Esq. 415-877-4486 of Rencher Law Group for Defendant AVRAHAM SARANGA.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT AVRAHAM SARANGA'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons.

Specially appearing defendant Avraham Saranga's motion to quash service of summons and complaint is denied. Substituted service per CCP 415.20(b) was properly made on Mr. Saranga. Plaintiff Cheryl Luna acted with reasonable diligence in trying to personally serve Mr. Saranga by attempting service at his home address nine times, including three occasions when a guard confirmed that Mr. Saranga lived in the apartment where personal service was attempted. Although the process server did not subserve a competent household member, leaving the papers with the guard on duty constituted substantial compliance with the statutory requirement because 1) the server could not go upstairs to attempt service on a household member since the guard repeatedly refused the server access to the upstairs, 2) the guard works in the building where Mr. Saranga resides and is more likely than not to know Mr. Saranga, 3) the process server mailed a copy of the summons and complaint to the specified address after subserving the guard, and 4) Mr. Saranga has had actual notice about this case since February 2018, when he retained counsel.

In addition, Defendant has 15 days to respond to the Complaint.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
